scope.summary: Persist conversation identity (title + avatar) so chat history shows the right name and avatar after a restart. The realtime listener message model (zca-rust TMessage) carries only a sender display name and NO avatar, so save_message always wrote thread_avatar = None and a group thread's title became the last sender. Avatars/names only exist in the directory data the app already fetches (list_contacts via get_all_friends, list_groups via get_group_info), which was never persisted. Fix: backfill existing persisted thread rows with the directory title + avatar in the store layer; the command layer calls the backfill right after it loads contacts/groups for an account. Only threads that already exist (real conversations) are enriched — directory-only contacts do not create thread rows. No new realtime data source and no IPC argument change (Tauri injects the store State).
goals: types/: ThreadIdentity DTO (camelCase) carries a resolved thread_id + title + avatar from command into the store backfill

store/: backfill_thread_identities(account_id, identities) updates title + avatar (COALESCE so a missing directory value keeps the existing one) for threads that already exist; returns the number of rows updated as a non-secret diagnostic
command/: list_contacts + list_groups persist directory identities (contact.user_id/display_name/avatar, group.group_id/name/avatar) onto existing thread rows after fetching
Offline test proves a saved thread gets its title + avatar backfilled and that a directory entry with no matching conversation does NOT create a thread row
load_history then returns threads carrying a persisted avatar + correct title on cold start before the live directory fetch resolves
nonGoals: Adding an avatar field to the realtime IncomingMessage / TMessage (upstream does not provide one on chat messages)
Persisting the full contact/group directory as its own table (only conversation identity is backfilled this slice)
Downloading/caching avatar image binaries to disk (URL only)
Any frontend/UI change (the store already feeds load_history, which the UI consumes)

acceptance.description: store/ backfills thread title + avatar for existing threads and skips directory entries with no matching conversation; the new offline test thread_identity_backfill_updates_existing_only passes and existing store tests still pass.
cargo clippy is clean with the new store method + command wiring.
command/ compiles: list_contacts/list_groups accept the store State and backfill without changing the IPC argument shape; no token values cross IPC.

Offline restart proof: save a thread, backfill its identity from directory data, drop + reopen the SQLite file (relaunch), and load_threads returns the persisted title + avatar with no realtime event and no live directory fetch.
